response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development across multiple domains and languages, including C/C++ (National Instruments role), but lacks direct experience with embedded networking security, Linux kernel modification, or networking technologies like MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, or switch chip knowledge. While proficient in Japanese (resume indicates German, but his background suggests adaptability), his technical focus appears more on web/mobile systems rather than embedded firmware or security features. His strengths in software craftsmanship and problem-solving are notable, but they do not fully align with the embedded networking security role.
strengths: Strong C/C++ experience (National Instruments, embedded-related work)
Proven software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, refactoring practices)
Multidisciplinary background (web, mobile, embedded, and tooling)
concerns: No direct experience with embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, tamper detection)
Limited exposure to Linux kernel modification or switch chip technologies
Lacks experience with networking protocols (REST API, NETCONF, SNMP) in an embedded context
